name: Eastweald
region: Eastern Lordaeron, the fertile pre-plaguelands region stretching beyond Andorhal toward the wider northern grain country.

sensoryProfile.sight: A child’s shoe half sunk in road mud, black-limbed orchard rows climbing low rises, shriveled fruit left unpicked, and northbound carts still loaded enough to prove ordinary life has not yet admitted what the road knows.
sensoryProfile.sound: Wheel-noise, refugees forcing traffic past one another, and the living road-sound of a region still working under disaster pressure.
sensoryProfile.smell: Cold road mud, tired horse sweat, autumn orchard rot, and smoke lying low from farther south.
sensoryProfile.touch: Deepened ruts, split cart wheels, saddle-jolt over damaged road, and the physical drag of traffic still trying to move households north.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: Chapter 15 turns Eastweald from ominous working country into visibly strained evacuation ground: still populated, still commercial, already giving way.

establishedFacts.fact: Eastweald is the next named field campaign zone after the Andorhal breakthrough.
The four-woman traveling unit will move into Eastweald while Corin and Havel remain local to Andorhal.
Kel'Thuzad is believed to be operating somewhere north of Andorhal, which points the widening hunt toward the Eastweald region without yet fixing a precise base.
North of Andorhal, the Eastweald roads stay clear, ditches are cut back, and dead orchards are trimmed for sightlines, proving the region is being actively maintained as hostile infrastructure.
Fresh ritual sites in Eastweald sit inside ordinary farm country, which lets plague work hide inside recognizably rural Lordaeron material instead of obvious ruin.
The ridge regroup establishes two actionable directions out of this part of Eastweald: Barov-held country to the east and the Hearthglen road to the south-west.
Chapter 10 keeps Eastweald outwardly prosperous on the Barov line: old-money roads, worked orchards, active labor, and estate traffic hiding the approach to live plague-support infrastructure.
Villages in Eastweald can still look orderly while already carrying plague administration through grain, watchers, and evidence control.
The south road toward Hearthglen carries both compromised grain-country pressure and the visible machinery of Lordaeron war response.
By chapter end Eastweald is also the northbound recovery road toward Darrowmere and Caer Darrow after Jaina’s trail goes silent.
The Eastweald road south of Darrowmere can still look like functioning farm country even while the protagonists carry knowledge that makes every loaf and wagon feel dangerous.
Ordinary wagon traffic, hedgerow farms, orchards, and evening bells remain active on the same road now feeding the Hearthglen and Stratholme crisis.
By chapter 14 Eastweald is no longer merely occupied countryside; it is the last stretch of apparent normalcy directly attached to the next urban catastrophe.
By chapter 15 Eastweald roads are carrying active refugee traffic north even before Hearthglen itself comes fully into view.
The region still looks materially ordinary enough to hide disaster in plain sight: orchards, farm carts, and market roads persist while the next city is already failing.

eventsHere.event: Chapter 8 names Eastweald as the next operational theater rather than a place already reached on page.
The field team investigates a fresh farmhouse ritual site and learns the plague mechanism is still being worked in active rural infrastructure.
A prepared compound perimeter north of Andorhal kills Lyriel and proves Eastweald is being engineered against disciplined scouts.
On a dusk ridge above the roads, Lor'themar forces the surviving team to split the Barov and Hearthglen lines apart.
Jaina rides the Barov line alone through maintained Eastweald estate country, confirming that the region's cultivated surface is now part of the enemy's concealment.
Sylvanas and Cyndia ride south through Eastweald, reading villages, crossroads, and shut granaries as proof that the plague is already embedded in ordinary countryside.
The chapter’s final turn sends Sylvanas and Cyndia north again through Eastweald toward Darrowmere once the Caer Darrow relay confirms Jaina has not returned.
Jaina briefs Sylvanas from the saddle while the party rides south through still-working Eastweald farm country, making the road itself the stage for strategic revelation and withheld truth.
A south-track relay on the Eastweald road confirms Hearthglen nearly failed again after dark and that Arthas is already racing Stratholme.
Sylvanas, Jaina, and Cyndia ride the refugee-clogged Eastweald road south toward Hearthglen and see visible proof that civilians are already fleeing loaded from the next line of collapse.
